value.0: The drow are an evil subrace of elves, living deep underground in the dangerous Underdark.

Like many evil items, these robes are fashioned in great numbers by the Zhentarim and their minions. They are found far and wide across the Realms, and the more hands they touch, the more influence the Zhents enjoy.
The drow are an evil subrace of elves, living deep underground in the dangerous Underdark.
This Talisman of Paladin was only awarded to those soldiers of light who reached the highest order of Avatar.
A Glowing Talisman with a Silver symbol of Paladine.

Like many evil items, these robes are fashioned in great numbers by the Zhentarim and their minions. They are found far and wide across the Realms, and the more hands they touch, the more influence the Zhents enjoy.

Feryl Dragonhunter was a monster of a man who, with four kills to his credit, thought he had perfected the art of dragon slaying. He aided his efforts by creating magical equipment from his previous victories, including several different boots he mockingly called his Dragon Slippers. Amazingly, Feryl was not defeated by his preferred enemy, but was surprised by a small band of goblins on his way to a hunt.

As age began to creep up on him, Beregost Hamish found it difficult to concentrate while casting challenging spells. The gloves he developed relaxed his mind in even the direst of situations. Before he died, Beregost made a fortune selling replicas of the original gloves to others in his chosen profession.
Greater Gloves of the Stone Heart
This Talisman of Paladin was only awarded to those soldiers of light who reached the highest order of Avatar.
A Glowing Talisman with a Silver symbol of Paladine.

This type of staff is the pinnacle of the artificer's craft, and each one represents the culmination of decades of study by a master spellcaster. With the secrets of their creation all but lost, more such staffs are found in the horde of ancient dragons than the hands of living wizards.
The drow are an evil subrace of elves, living deep underground in the dangerous Underdark.

Having explored the northlands extensively, a druid named Aawill commissioned the creation of these comfortable boots. Knowing he was heading for the sometimes-dangerous domain of the Neverwinter Woods, Aawill was looking for something helpful in combat, to lighten his need for armor. Since becoming the Archdruid for the Neverwinter Wood's Druid Grove, Aawill has commissioned several, more powerful versions of his original boots.
This Upgraded version of the Dagger of Stars has in addition to the Invisibility ability. Been enchanted to do extra elemental damage.

The lowly wizard Huhhus discovered an efficient way to distill troll flesh into a regenerative potion, but few people were willing to drink the vile liquid and it was considered a failure. Decades later, one of his students built upon these foundations to create a regenerative ring fueled by bits of troll ichor and brains. This external use caused far less nausea, and the invention met with success.
The drow are an evil subrace of elves, living deep underground in the dangerous Underdark.

Hoisure the Prudent was a cunning pirate who sought every advantage he could for his men. He commissioned a number of these belts at great expense, and with his men so equipped quickly took control of great tracts of coastline. Although he ruled with remarkable restraint and wisdom for many years, eventually his enemies gained the upper hand, and he remains a villain in the eyes of history.
This arrow not only pierces its target, it also releases a splash of alchemical acid on impact.
